name: Cisco Meraki Dashboard API
what_it_does: Cisco Meraki Dashboard REST API for cloud-managed networking platform. Enables AI agents to manage network device inventory and configuration, handle client and device analytics and monitoring, access network traffic analytics and application visibility, retrieve security event and intrusion detection data, manage SSID and wireless configuration automation, handle VLAN and switching configuration management, access site-to-site VPN and SD-WAN configuration, retrieve real-time client connection and location data, manage Meraki camera and sensor data integration, and integrate Meraki network data with ITSM, SIEM, and network operations platforms.
use_cases: Monitoring network client health from network operations agents
Automating SSID and wireless policy from IT infrastructure agents
Accessing security appliance threat events from security operations agents
Integrating network analytics with ITSM from IT operations agents
not_for: Enterprise data center networking without cloud-managed simplicity
Carrier-grade networking without SMB and mid-market cloud management
Telecommunications routing without enterprise campus network focus
best_when: An organization using Cisco Meraki wants AI agents to automate network monitoring, device configuration, client analytics, security event processing, and ITSM integration.
avoid_when: OPERATIONAL RISK: Automated network configuration changes can cause network outages — always use Meraki simulation mode and change management windows. Wireless configuration automation (SSID changes) affects all connected clients immediately.

auth.notes: Meraki uses API key authentication with X-Cisco-Meraki-API-Key header. Organization-level API keys with read/write access. Comprehensive developer documentation at developer.cisco.com/meraki. Webhooks for network events. SDKs for Python, Go, Node.js. Postman collection available. Meraki Scanning API for real-time location data. Rate limiting with throttling.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free API fee — API included with Meraki hardware and licensing
pricing.paid_tiers: Meraki hardware + annual license; Enterprise license required for API and advanced features
pricing.notes: San Francisco, California. Cisco Systems (acquired 2012, $1.2B). Cloud-managed networking leader. 750,000+ customers. Strong SMB, mid-market, and distributed enterprise. MR (wireless), MS (switching), MX (security appliance), MV (cameras), MT (sensors). Meraki Dashboard for centralized management. Competes with Aruba and Juniper Mist for cloud-managed networking.

performance.rate_limits: 10 API calls/second; max 5 concurrent calls per API key

known_agent_gotchas: OPERATIONAL RISK: Configuration changes take effect immediately on network devices — always test in dev/lab environment; use change management windows
Rate limit strictly enforced — 10 calls/sec and 5 concurrent; implement token bucket with backoff for bulk operations
API key only — no OAuth or granular scopes; API key has read/write org access; restrict key storage carefully
Pagination required — list endpoints paginate with startingAfter cursor; implement pagination for all list queries
Meraki Scanning API separate — real-time location/presence uses MQTT-based Scanning API; different from REST Dashboard API
Hardware lifecycle dependency — API functionality depends on hardware model; older MX appliances may not support newer API features
agent_readiness.security_notes: Cloud-managed networking. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. API key. Multi-region. Network configuration and client data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cloud networking. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. API key. Multi-region. Network configuration and client connectivity data.
